lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
Eusebius having convened Another Synod at Antioch in Syria, causes a New Creed to be promulgated.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Eusebius having convened Another Synod at Antioch in Syria, causes a New Creed to be promulgated.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Eusebius, however, could by no means remain quiet, but as the saying is, left no stone unturned, in order to effect the purpose he had in view.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He therefore causes a Synod to be convened at Antioch in Syria, under pretense of dedicating the church which the father of the Augusti had commenced, and which his son Constantius had finished in the tenth year after its foundations were laid, but with the real intention of subverting and abolishing the doctrine of the homoousion.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: There were present at this Synod ninety bishops from various cities.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Maximus, however, bishop of Jerusalem; who had succeeded Macarius, did not attend, recollecting that he had been deceived and induced to subscribe the deposition of Athanasius.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Neither was Julius, bishop of the great Rome, there, nor had he sent a substitute, although an ecclesiastical canon commands that the churches shall not make any ordinances against the opinion of the bishop of Rome.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This Synod assembled at Antioch in presence of the emperor Constantius in the consulate of Marcellus and Probinus, which was the fifth year after the death of Constantine, father of the Augusti.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Placitus, otherwise called Flaccillus, successor to Euphronius, at that time presided over the church at Antioch.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The confederates of Eusebius had previously designed to calumniate Athanasius; accusing him in the first place of having acted contrary to a canon which they then constituted, in resuming his episcopal authority without the license of a general council of bishops, inasmuch as on his return from exile he had on his own responsibility taken possession of the church; and then because a tumult had been excited on his entrance and many were killed in the riot; moreover that some had been scourged by him, and others brought before the tribunals.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Besides they brought forward what had been determined against Athanasius at Tyre.
